title: Wall divided by a curvy line — retry, flowing streamlines

rule.instruction: A wall divided from the upper left to the lower right by a curvy line; left: glossy yellow; right: glossy purple.

restrictions: RETRY of #852 after round 24 was rejected ('all look similar, not much worth'). Same monochrome adaptation (curvy corner-to-corner divide; two sides contrasted by direction, no colour), but the flat straight 'grain' is replaced by FLOWING STREAMLINES integrated through a swirled flow field (the round-11 directional-flow that scored highest) — each side a contrasting current, the curvy seam the hero. Variety pushed hard across the 16: curve shape (near-straight steep / gentle / big single hump / S-curve / deep multi-wave), flow swirl (calm -> very swirly), angle contrast, and openness. Monochrome; hand-made; dense areas are flow (dynamic), not mechanical fill. `curvyDivide` extended with a `swirl` flow-field.
